speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: So now prosperity begins to mellow

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And drop into the rotten mouth of death.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Here in these confines slyly have I lurked

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: To watch the waning of mine enemies.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: A dire induction am I witness to,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And will to France, hoping the consequence

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Will prove as bitter, black, and tragical.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Withdraw thee, wretched Margaret. Who comes here?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Ah, my poor princes! Ah, my tender babes,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: My unblown flowers, new-appearing sweets,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: If yet your gentle souls fly in the air

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And be not fixed in doom perpetual,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Hover about me with your airy wings

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And hear your mother’s lamentation.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Hover about her; say that right for right

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Hath dimmed your infant morn to agèd night.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: So many miseries have crazed my voice

speaker: DUCHESS
text: That my woe-wearied tongue is still and mute.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Edward Plantagenet, why art thou dead?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Plantagenet doth quit Plantagenet;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Edward for Edward pays a dying debt.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Wilt thou, O God, fly from such gentle lambs

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And throw them in the entrails of the wolf?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: When didst thou sleep when such a deed was done?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: When holy Harry died, and my sweet son.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Dead life, blind sight, poor mortal living ghost,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Woe’s scene, world’s shame, grave’s due by life usurped,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Brief abstract and record of tedious days,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Rest thy unrest on England’s lawful earth,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Unlawfully made drunk with innocent blood.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Ah, that thou wouldst as soon afford a grave

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: As thou canst yield a melancholy seat,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Then would I hide my bones, not rest them here.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Ah, who hath any cause to mourn but we?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: If ancient sorrow be most reverend,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Give mine the benefit of seigniory,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And let my griefs frown on the upper hand.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: If sorrow can admit society,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Tell over your woes again by viewing mine.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I had an Edward till a Richard killed him;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I had a husband till a Richard killed him.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thou hadst an Edward till a Richard killed him;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thou hadst a Richard till a Richard killed him.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I had a Richard too, and thou did’st kill him;

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I had a Rutland too; thou holp’st to kill him.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thou hadst a Clarence too, and Richard killed him.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: From forth the kennel of thy womb hath crept

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: A hellhound that doth hunt us all to death—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That dog, that had his teeth before his eyes,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: To worry lambs and lap their gentle blood;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That excellent grand tyrant of the Earth,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That reigns in gallèd eyes of weeping souls;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That foul defacer of God’s handiwork

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thy womb let loose to chase us to our graves.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: O upright, just, and true-disposing God,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: How do I thank thee that this carnal cur

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Preys on the issue of his mother’s body

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And makes her pew-fellow with others’ moan!

speaker: DUCHESS
text: O Harry’s wife, triumph not in my woes!

speaker: DUCHESS
text: God witness with me, I have wept for thine.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Bear with me. I am hungry for revenge,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And now I cloy me with beholding it.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thy Edward he is dead, that killed my Edward,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thy other Edward dead, to quit my Edward;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Young York, he is but boot, because both they

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Matched not the high perfection of my loss.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thy Clarence he is dead that stabbed my Edward,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And the beholders of this frantic play,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Th’ adulterate Hastings, Rivers, Vaughan, Grey,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Untimely smothered in their dusky graves.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Richard yet lives, hell’s black intelligencer,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Only reserved their factor to buy souls

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And send them thither. But at hand, at hand

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Ensues his piteous and unpitied end.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Earth gapes, hell burns, fiends roar, saints pray,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: To have him suddenly conveyed from hence.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Cancel his bond of life, dear God I pray,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That I may live and say “The dog is dead.”

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: O, thou didst prophesy the time would come

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: That I should wish for thee to help me curse

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: That bottled spider, that foul bunch-backed toad!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I called thee then “vain flourish of my fortune.”

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I called thee then poor shadow, “painted queen,”

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The presentation of but what I was,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The flattering index of a direful pageant,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: One heaved a-high to be hurled down below,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: A mother only mocked with two fair babes,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: A dream of what thou wast, a garish flag

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: To be the aim of every dangerous shot,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: A sign of dignity, a breath, a bubble,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: A queen in jest, only to fill the scene.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Where is thy husband now? Where be thy brothers?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Where are thy two sons? Wherein dost thou joy?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Who sues and kneels and says “God save the Queen?”

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Where be the bending peers that flattered thee?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Where be the thronging troops that followed thee?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Decline all this, and see what now thou art:

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For happy wife, a most distressèd widow;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For joyful mother, one that wails the name;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For one being sued to, one that humbly sues;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For queen, a very caitiff crowned with care;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For she that scorned at me, now scorned of me;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For she being feared of all, now fearing one;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For she commanding all, obeyed of none.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thus hath the course of justice whirled about

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And left thee but a very prey to time,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Having no more but thought of what thou wast

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: To torture thee the more, being what thou art.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thou didst usurp my place, and dost thou not

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Usurp the just proportion of my sorrow?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Now thy proud neck bears half my burdened yoke,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: From which even here I slip my weary head

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And leave the burden of it all on thee.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Farewell, York’s wife, and queen of sad mischance.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: These English woes shall make me smile in France.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: O, thou well-skilled in curses, stay awhile,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And teach me how to curse mine enemies.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Forbear to sleep the nights, and fast the days;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Compare dead happiness with living woe;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Think that thy babes were sweeter than they were,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And he that slew them fouler than he is.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Bettering thy loss makes the bad causer worse.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Revolving this will teach thee how to curse.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: My words are dull. O, quicken them with thine!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thy woes will make them sharp and pierce like mine.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Why should calamity be full of words?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Windy attorneys to their clients’ woes,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Airy succeeders of intestate joys,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Poor breathing orators of miseries,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Let them have scope; though what they will impart

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Help nothing else, yet do they ease the heart.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: If so, then be not tongue-tied. Go with me,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And in the breath of bitter words let’s smother

speaker: DUCHESS
text: My damnèd son that thy two sweet sons smothered.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: The trumpet sounds. Be copious in exclaims.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Who intercepts me in my expedition?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: O, she that might have intercepted thee,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: By strangling thee in her accursèd womb,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: From all the slaughters, wretch, that thou hast done.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Hid’st thou that forehead with a golden crown

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Where should be branded, if that right were right,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: The slaughter of the prince that owed that crown

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And the dire death of my poor sons and brothers?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Tell me, thou villain-slave, where are my children?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Thou toad, thou toad, where is thy brother Clarence,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And little Ned Plantagenet his son?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Where is the gentle Rivers, Vaughan, Grey?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Where is kind Hastings?

speaker: RICHARD
text: A flourish, trumpets! Strike alarum, drums!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Let not the heavens hear these telltale women

speaker: RICHARD
text: Rail on the Lord’s anointed. Strike, I say!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Either be patient and entreat me fair,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Or with the clamorous report of war

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thus will I drown your exclamations.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Art thou my son?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ay, I thank God, my father, and yourself.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Then patiently hear my impatience.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Madam, I have a touch of your condition,

speaker: RICHARD
text: That cannot brook the accent of reproof.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: O, let me speak!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Do then, but I’ll not hear.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I will be mild and gentle in my words.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And brief, good mother, for I am in haste.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Art thou so hasty? I have stayed for thee,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: God knows, in torment and in agony.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And came I not at last to comfort you?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: No, by the Holy Rood, thou know’st it well.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Thou cam’st on Earth to make the Earth my hell.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: A grievous burden was thy birth to me;

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Tetchy and wayward was thy infancy;

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Thy school days frightful, desp’rate, wild, and furious;

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Thy prime of manhood daring, bold, and venturous;

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Thy age confirmed, proud, subtle, sly, and bloody,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: More mild, but yet more harmful, kind in hatred.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: What comfortable hour canst thou name,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: That ever graced me with thy company?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Faith, none but Humfrey Hower, that called your Grace

speaker: RICHARD
text: To breakfast once, forth of my company.

speaker: RICHARD
text: If I be so disgracious in your eye,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Let me march on and not offend you, madam.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Strike up the drum.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I prithee, hear me speak.

speaker: RICHARD
text: You speak too bitterly.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Hear me a word,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: For I shall never speak to thee again.

speaker: RICHARD
text: So.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Either thou wilt die by God’s just ordinance

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Ere from this war thou turn a conqueror,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Or I with grief and extreme age shall perish

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And nevermore behold thy face again.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Therefore take with thee my most grievous curse,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Which in the day of battle tire thee more

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Than all the complete armor that thou wear’st.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: My prayers on the adverse party fight,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And there the little souls of Edward’s children

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Whisper the spirits of thine enemies

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And promise them success and victory.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Bloody thou art; bloody will be thy end.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Shame serves thy life and doth thy death attend.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Though far more cause, yet much less spirit to curse

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Abides in me. I say amen to her.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Stay, madam. I must talk a word with you.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: I have no more sons of the royal blood

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: For thee to slaughter. For my daughters, Richard,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: They shall be praying nuns, not weeping queens,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And therefore level not to hit their lives.

speaker: RICHARD
text: You have a daughter called Elizabeth,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Virtuous and fair, royal and gracious.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And must she die for this? O, let her live,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And I’ll corrupt her manners, stain her beauty,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Slander myself as false to Edward’s bed,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Throw over her the veil of infamy.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: So she may live unscarred of bleeding slaughter,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: I will confess she was not Edward’s daughter.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Wrong not her birth. She is a royal princess.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: To save her life, I’ll say she is not so.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Her life is safest only in her birth.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And only in that safety died her brothers.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Lo, at their birth good stars were opposite.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: No, to their lives ill friends were contrary.

speaker: RICHARD
text: All unavoided is the doom of destiny.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: True, when avoided grace makes destiny.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: My babes were destined to a fairer death

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: If grace had blessed thee with a fairer life.

speaker: RICHARD
text: You speak as if that I had slain my cousins.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Cousins, indeed, and by their uncle cozened

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Of comfort, kingdom, kindred, freedom, life.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Whose hand soever launched their tender hearts,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Thy head, all indirectly, gave direction.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: No doubt the murd’rous knife was dull and blunt

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Till it was whetted on thy stone-hard heart,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: To revel in the entrails of my lambs.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: But that still use of grief makes wild grief tame,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: My tongue should to thy ears not name my boys

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Till that my nails were anchored in thine eyes,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And I, in such a desp’rate bay of death,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Like a poor bark of sails and tackling reft,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Rush all to pieces on thy rocky bosom.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Madam, so thrive I in my enterprise

speaker: RICHARD
text: And dangerous success of bloody wars

speaker: RICHARD
text: As I intend more good to you and yours

speaker: RICHARD
text: Than ever you or yours by me were harmed!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: What good is covered with the face of heaven,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: To be discovered, that can do me good?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Th’ advancement of your children, gentle lady.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Up to some scaffold, there to lose their heads.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Unto the dignity and height of fortune,

speaker: RICHARD
text: The high imperial type of this Earth’s glory.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Flatter my sorrow with report of it.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Tell me what state, what dignity, what honor,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Canst thou demise to any child of mine?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Even all I have—ay, and myself and all—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Will I withal endow a child of thine;

speaker: RICHARD
text: So in the Lethe of thy angry soul

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thou drown the sad remembrance of those wrongs

speaker: RICHARD
text: Which thou supposest I have done to thee.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Be brief, lest that the process of thy kindness

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Last longer telling than thy kindness’ date.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then know that from my soul I love thy daughter.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: My daughter’s mother thinks it with her soul.

speaker: RICHARD
text: What do you think?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: That thou dost love my daughter from thy soul.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: So from thy soul’s love didst thou love her brothers,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And from my heart’s love I do thank thee for it.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Be not so hasty to confound my meaning.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I mean that with my soul I love thy daughter

speaker: RICHARD
text: And do intend to make her Queen of England.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Well then, who dost thou mean shall be her king?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Even he that makes her queen. Who else should be?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: What, thou?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Even so. How think you of it?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: How canst thou woo her?

speaker: RICHARD
text: That would I learn of you,

speaker: RICHARD
text: As one being best acquainted with her humor.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And wilt thou learn of me?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Madam, with all my heart.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Send to her, by the man that slew her brothers,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: A pair of bleeding hearts; thereon engrave

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: “Edward” and “York.” Then haply will she weep.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Therefore present to her—as sometime Margaret

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Did to thy father, steeped in Rutland’s blood—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: A handkerchief, which say to her did drain

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: The purple sap from her sweet brother’s body,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And bid her wipe her weeping eyes withal.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: If this inducement move her not to love,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Send her a letter of thy noble deeds;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Tell her thou mad’st away her uncle Clarence,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Her uncle Rivers, ay, and for her sake

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Mad’st quick conveyance with her good aunt Anne.

speaker: RICHARD
text: You mock me, madam. This is not the way

speaker: RICHARD
text: To win your daughter.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: There is no other way,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Unless thou couldst put on some other shape

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And not be Richard, that hath done all this.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Say that I did all this for love of her.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Nay, then indeed she cannot choose but hate thee,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Having bought love with such a bloody spoil.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Look what is done cannot be now amended.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Men shall deal unadvisedly sometimes,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Which after-hours gives leisure to repent.

speaker: RICHARD
text: If I did take the kingdom from your sons,

speaker: RICHARD
text: To make amends I’ll give it to your daughter.

speaker: RICHARD
text: If I have killed the issue of your womb,

speaker: RICHARD
text: To quicken your increase I will beget

speaker: RICHARD
text: Mine issue of your blood upon your daughter.

speaker: RICHARD
text: A grandam’s name is little less in love

speaker: RICHARD
text: Than is the doting title of a mother.

speaker: RICHARD
text: They are as children but one step below,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Even of your metal, of your very blood,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Of all one pain, save for a night of groans

speaker: RICHARD
text: Endured of her for whom you bid like sorrow.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Your children were vexation to your youth,

speaker: RICHARD
text: But mine shall be a comfort to your age.

speaker: RICHARD
text: The loss you have is but a son being king,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And by that loss your daughter is made queen.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I cannot make you what amends I would;

speaker: RICHARD
text: Therefore accept such kindness as I can.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Dorset your son, that with a fearful soul

speaker: RICHARD
text: Leads discontented steps in foreign soil,

speaker: RICHARD
text: This fair alliance quickly shall call home

speaker: RICHARD
text: To high promotions and great dignity.

speaker: RICHARD
text: The king that calls your beauteous daughter wife

speaker: RICHARD
text: Familiarly shall call thy Dorset brother.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Again shall you be mother to a king,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And all the ruins of distressful times

speaker: RICHARD
text: Repaired with double riches of content.

speaker: RICHARD
text: What, we have many goodly days to see!

speaker: RICHARD
text: The liquid drops of tears that you have shed

speaker: RICHARD
text: Shall come again, transformed to orient pearl,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Advantaging their love with interest

speaker: RICHARD
text: Of ten times double gain of happiness.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Go then, my mother; to thy daughter go.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Make bold her bashful years with your experience;

speaker: RICHARD
text: Prepare her ears to hear a wooer’s tale;

speaker: RICHARD
text: Put in her tender heart th’ aspiring flame

speaker: RICHARD
text: Of golden sovereignty; acquaint the Princess

speaker: RICHARD
text: With the sweet silent hours of marriage joys;

speaker: RICHARD
text: And when this arm of mine hath chastisèd

speaker: RICHARD
text: The petty rebel, dull-brained Buckingham,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Bound with triumphant garlands will I come

speaker: RICHARD
text: And lead thy daughter to a conqueror’s bed,

speaker: RICHARD
text: To whom I will retail my conquest won,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And she shall be sole victoress, Caesar’s Caesar.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: What were I best to say? Her father’s brother

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Would be her lord? Or shall I say her uncle?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Or he that slew her brothers and her uncles?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Under what title shall I woo for thee,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: That God, the law, my honor, and her love

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Can make seem pleasing to her tender years?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Infer fair England’s peace by this alliance.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Which she shall purchase with still-lasting war.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Tell her the King, that may command, entreats—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: That, at her hands, which the King’s King forbids.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Say she shall be a high and mighty queen.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: To vail the title, as her mother doth.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Say I will love her everlastingly.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: But how long shall that title “ever” last?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Sweetly in force unto her fair life’s end.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: But how long fairly shall her sweet life last?

speaker: RICHARD
text: As long as heaven and nature lengthens it.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: As long as hell and Richard likes of it.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Say I, her sovereign, am her subject low.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: But she, your subject, loathes such sovereignty.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Be eloquent in my behalf to her.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: An honest tale speeds best being plainly told.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then plainly to her tell my loving tale.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Plain and not honest is too harsh a style.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Your reasons are too shallow and too quick.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: O no, my reasons are too deep and dead—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Too deep and dead, poor infants, in their graves.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Harp not on that string, madam; that is past.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Harp on it still shall I till heart-strings break.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Now by my George, my Garter, and my crown—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Profaned, dishonored, and the third usurped.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I swear—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: By nothing, for this is no oath.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Thy George, profaned, hath lost his lordly honor;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Thy Garter, blemished, pawned his knightly virtue;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Thy crown, usurped, disgraced his kingly glory.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: If something thou wouldst swear to be believed,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Swear then by something that thou hast not wronged.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then, by myself—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Thyself is self-misused.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Now, by the world—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: ’Tis full of thy foul wrongs.

speaker: RICHARD
text: My father’s death—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Thy life hath it dishonored.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Why then, by God.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: God’s wrong is most of all.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: If thou didst fear to break an oath with Him,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: The unity the King my husband made

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Thou hadst not broken, nor my brothers died.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: If thou hadst feared to break an oath by Him,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Th’ imperial metal circling now thy head

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Had graced the tender temples of my child,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And both the Princes had been breathing here,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Which now, two tender bedfellows for dust,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Thy broken faith hath made the prey for worms.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: What canst thou swear by now?

speaker: RICHARD
text: The time to come.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: That thou hast wrongèd in the time o’erpast;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: For I myself have many tears to wash

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Hereafter time, for time past wronged by thee.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: The children live whose fathers thou hast slaughtered,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Ungoverned youth, to wail it in their age;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: The parents live whose children thou hast butchered,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Old barren plants, to wail it with their age.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Swear not by time to come, for that thou hast

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Misused ere used, by times ill-used o’erpast.

speaker: RICHARD
text: As I intend to prosper and repent,

speaker: RICHARD
text: So thrive I in my dangerous affairs

speaker: RICHARD
text: Of hostile arms! Myself myself confound,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Heaven and fortune bar me happy hours,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Day, yield me not thy light, nor night thy rest,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Be opposite all planets of good luck

speaker: RICHARD
text: To my proceeding if, with dear heart’s love,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Immaculate devotion, holy thoughts,

speaker: RICHARD
text: I tender not thy beauteous princely daughter.

speaker: RICHARD
text: In her consists my happiness and thine.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Without her follows to myself and thee,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Herself, the land, and many a Christian soul,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Death, desolation, ruin, and decay.

speaker: RICHARD
text: It cannot be avoided but by this;

speaker: RICHARD
text: It will not be avoided but by this.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Therefore, dear mother—I must call you so—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Be the attorney of my love to her;

speaker: RICHARD
text: Plead what I will be, not what I have been;

speaker: RICHARD
text: Not my deserts, but what I will deserve.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Urge the necessity and state of times,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And be not peevish found in great designs.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Shall I be tempted of the devil thus?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ay, if the devil tempt you to do good.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Shall I forget myself to be myself?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ay, if your self’s remembrance wrong yourself.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Yet thou didst kill my children.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But in your daughter’s womb I bury them,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Where, in that nest of spicery, they will breed

speaker: RICHARD
text: Selves of themselves, to your recomforture.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Shall I go win my daughter to thy will?

speaker: RICHARD
text: And be a happy mother by the deed.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: I go. Write to me very shortly,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And you shall understand from me her mind.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Bear her my true love’s kiss; and so, farewell.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Relenting fool and shallow, changing woman!

speaker: RICHARD
text: How now, what news?

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Most mighty sovereign, on the western coast

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Rideth a puissant navy. To our shores

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Throng many doubtful hollow-hearted friends,

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Unarmed and unresolved to beat them back.

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: ’Tis thought that Richmond is their admiral;

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: And there they hull, expecting but the aid

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Of Buckingham to welcome them ashore.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Some light-foot friend post to the Duke of Norfolk—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ratcliffe thyself, or Catesby. Where is he?

speaker: CATESBY
text: Here, my good lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Catesby, fly to the Duke.

speaker: CATESBY
text: I will, my lord, with all convenient haste.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ratcliffe, come hither. Post to Salisbury.

speaker: RICHARD
text:
When thou com’st thither—
                To
                 
                Catesby
                .
                 
              Dull, unmindful villain,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Why stay’st thou here and go’st not to the Duke?

speaker: CATESBY
text: First, mighty liege, tell me your Highness’ pleasure,

speaker: CATESBY
text: What from your Grace I shall deliver to him.

speaker: RICHARD
text: O true, good Catesby. Bid him levy straight

speaker: RICHARD
text: The greatest strength and power that he can make

speaker: RICHARD
text: And meet me suddenly at Salisbury.

speaker: CATESBY
text: I go.

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: What, may it please you, shall I do at Salisbury?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Why, what wouldst thou do there before I go?

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Your Highness told me I should post before.

speaker: RICHARD
text: My mind is changed.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Stanley, what news with you?

speaker: STANLEY
text: None good, my liege, to please you with the hearing,

speaker: STANLEY
text: Nor none so bad but well may be reported.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Hoyday, a riddle! Neither good nor bad.

speaker: RICHARD
text: What need’st thou run so many miles about

speaker: RICHARD
text: When thou mayst tell thy tale the nearest way?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Once more, what news?

speaker: STANLEY
text: Richmond is on the seas.

speaker: RICHARD
text: There let him sink, and be the seas on him!

speaker: RICHARD
text: White-livered runagate, what doth he there?

speaker: STANLEY
text: I know not, mighty sovereign, but by guess.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Well, as you guess?

speaker: STANLEY
text: Stirred up by Dorset, Buckingham, and Morton,

speaker: STANLEY
text: He makes for England, here to claim the crown.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Is the chair empty? Is the sword unswayed?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Is the King dead, the empire unpossessed?

speaker: RICHARD
text: What heir of York is there alive but we?

speaker: RICHARD
text: And who is England’s king but great York’s heir?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then tell me, what makes he upon the seas?

speaker: STANLEY
text: Unless for that, my liege, I cannot guess.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Unless for that he comes to be your liege,

speaker: RICHARD
text: You cannot guess wherefore the Welshman comes.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thou wilt revolt and fly to him, I fear.

speaker: STANLEY
text: No, my good lord. Therefore mistrust me not.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Where is thy power, then, to beat him back?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Where be thy tenants and thy followers?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Are they not now upon the western shore,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Safe-conducting the rebels from their ships?

speaker: STANLEY
text: No, my good lord. My friends are in the north.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Cold friends to me. What do they in the north

speaker: RICHARD
text: When they should serve their sovereign in the west?

speaker: STANLEY
text: They have not been commanded, mighty king.

speaker: STANLEY
text: Pleaseth your Majesty to give me leave,

speaker: STANLEY
text: I’ll muster up my friends and meet your Grace

speaker: STANLEY
text: Where and what time your Majesty shall please.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ay, thou wouldst be gone to join with Richmond,

speaker: RICHARD
text: But I’ll not trust thee.

speaker: STANLEY
text: Most mighty sovereign,

speaker: STANLEY
text: You have no cause to hold my friendship doubtful.

speaker: STANLEY
text: I never was nor never will be false.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Go then and muster men, but leave behind

speaker: RICHARD
text: Your son George Stanley. Look your heart be firm,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Or else his head’s assurance is but frail.

speaker: STANLEY
text: So deal with him as I prove true to you.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MESSENGER
text: My gracious sovereign, now in Devonshire,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MESSENGER
text: As I by friends am well advertisèd,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MESSENGER
text: Sir Edward Courtney and the haughty prelate,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MESSENGER
text: Bishop of Exeter, his elder brother,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MESSENGER
text: With many more confederates are in arms.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: In Kent, my liege, the Guilfords are in arms,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: And every hour more competitors

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: Flock to the rebels, and their power grows strong.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: My lord, the army of great Buckingham—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Out on you, owls! Nothing but songs of death.

speaker: RICHARD
text: There, take thou that till thou bring better news.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: The news I have to tell your Majesty

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Is that by sudden floods and fall of waters

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Buckingham’s army is dispersed and scattered,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: And he himself wandered away alone,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: No man knows whither.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I cry thee mercy.

speaker: RICHARD
text: There is my purse to cure that blow of thine.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Hath any well-advisèd friend proclaimed

speaker: RICHARD
text: Reward to him that brings the traitor in?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Such proclamation hath been made, my lord.

speaker:
FOURTH
               
              MESSENGER
text: Sir Thomas Lovell and Lord Marquess Dorset,

speaker:
FOURTH
               
              MESSENGER
text: ’Tis said, my liege, in Yorkshire are in arms.

speaker:
FOURTH
               
              MESSENGER
text: But this good comfort bring I to your Highness:

speaker:
FOURTH
               
              MESSENGER
text: The Breton navy is dispersed by tempest.

speaker:
FOURTH
               
              MESSENGER
text: Richmond, in Dorsetshire, sent out a boat

speaker:
FOURTH
               
              MESSENGER
text: Unto the shore to ask those on the banks

speaker:
FOURTH
               
              MESSENGER
text: If they were his assistants, yea, or no—

speaker:
FOURTH
               
              MESSENGER
text: Who answered him they came from Buckingham

speaker:
FOURTH
               
              MESSENGER
text: Upon his party. He, mistrusting them,

speaker:
FOURTH
               
              MESSENGER
text: Hoised sail and made his course again for Brittany.

speaker: RICHARD
text: March on, march on, since we are up in arms,

speaker: RICHARD
text: If not to fight with foreign enemies,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Yet to beat down these rebels here at home.

speaker: CATESBY
text: My liege, the Duke of Buckingham is taken.

speaker: CATESBY
text: That is the best news. That the Earl of Richmond

speaker: CATESBY
text: Is with a mighty power landed at Milford

speaker: CATESBY
text: Is colder tidings, yet they must be told.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Away towards Salisbury! While we reason here,

speaker: RICHARD
text: A royal battle might be won and lost.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Someone take order Buckingham be brought

speaker: RICHARD
text: To Salisbury. The rest march on with me.
